outbound_official.note: No official Panama government outbound visa-list page located. Checked: mire.gob.pa (Ministry of Foreign Affairs - no outbound list, navigation has no such section), apap.gob.pa (Passport Authority - SSL certificate errors blocked access), migracion.gob.pa (inbound immigration only), panamadigital.gob.pa (inbound only), sigob.mire.gob.pa/consular (DB error). Tocumen International Airport (state entity) hosts a page titled 'Acuerdo de Supresión de Visas' at tocumenpanama.aero/index.php/acuerdo-de-supresion-de-visas but the country list is JavaScript-rendered and could not be extracted. Rely on destination-side data.

name: Panama

visa_types.name: Tourist Entry (Visa-Free / Visa Exemption)
visa_types.purpose: Short-term tourism and leisure for nationals of exempt countries. Entry is granted at the border without prior application.
visa_types.notes: USA and Canada nationals receive up to 180 days; other exempt nationalities (100+ countries) receive up to 90 days under Resolution 22706. Minimum passport validity 3 months. Must show proof of economic solvency (B/.500 / USD 500) and a return ticket. Nationals of non-exempt countries must obtain a stamped or authorized visa from a consulate before travel.

visa_types.name: Visa Estampada (Stamped Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Tourist/visitor visa for nationals of countries not covered by visa-free exemptions. Obtained at a Panamanian consulate before travel.
visa_types.notes: Fee is USD 50 application + USD 90 (Western Union); total ~USD 140. Processing up to 30 business days. Requires: visa application form, passport copies, flight reservation, government-issued ID, 3 passport photos, proof of funds (USD 500 minimum), hotel reservation. Must apply in person at nearest Panamanian consulate.

visa_types.name: Visa Autorizada (Authorized Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Tourist/visitor visa for nationals requiring prior SNM (National Migration Service) authorization, typically where no local consulate is available.
visa_types.notes: Fee is USD 50 application + USD 90 (Western Union); total ~USD 140. Processing up to 60 business days. Same documentation as stamped visa. Authorization is granted by SNM headquarters; applicant must then present authorization at port of entry.

visa_types.name: Visa de Tránsito (Transit Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Permits foreign nationals to transit through Panama en route to another destination without entering the country proper.
visa_types.notes: Required for nationals of countries that do not qualify for visa-free transit. Exemptions exist for holders of valid Panamanian visas or qualifying third-country visas (US, Canada, UK, EU, Japan, South Korea, Australia, Singapore). Must hold onward travel documentation. As of 2025 Decrees 21 and 22 (August 2025), transit visa requirements were amended for Haitian and Cuban nationals.

visa_types.name: Visa de Corta Estancia - Negocios (Short-Stay Business Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Permits short-term business activities in Panama such as meetings, negotiations, and conferences without taking up local employment.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a non-resident short-stay visa category on the SNM Permisos Migratorios page. Specific fee and processing time not published on official web pages; requirements are available as downloadable PDFs from migracion.gob.pa.

visa_types.name: Visa de Corta Estancia como Trabajador Remoto (Short-Stay Remote Worker Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign remote workers employed by or contracted with foreign transnational companies to live and work remotely from Panama for up to 18 months.
visa_types.notes: Created by Executive Decree 198 of May 7, 2021. Initial stay 9 months, renewable once for a further 9 months (total maximum 18 months). Minimum annual income requirement: B/.36,000 (USD 36,000) from foreign sources. Applicant must be physically present in Panama to apply at SNM Special Processing Window. Must not work for Panamanian companies or local clients. Foreign-source income is not subject to Panamanian income tax under Panama's territorial tax system. Also referenced on ProPanama official site.

visa_types.name: Permiso de Trabajo Temporal - Trabajador Técnico/Temporal (V-TET) (Temporary/Technical Worker Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Non-resident permit for foreign technical or temporary workers engaged by Panamanian employers for a defined project or term.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a non-resident work-related permit on the SNM Permisos Migratorios page. Specific fees and processing times are in downloadable requirement PDFs on migracion.gob.pa. Other non-resident work permits include those for sailors, entertainment workers, domestic workers, transient/event workers, banking professionals, and special commercial/investor categories.

visa_types.name: Permiso de Trabajo - Representante de Empresa Multinacional (Multinational Company Representative Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency permit for executives and specialists working for multinational companies headquartered or operating in Panama's Multinational Company Headquarters (SEM) regime.
visa_types.notes: Temporary resident category under Panama's SEM (Sedes de Empresas Multinacionales) law. Companies must be registered under Law 41 of 2007. Includes accompanying dependents. Processing handled by SNM.

visa_types.name: Permiso de Residente Temporal por Contrato con el Estado (Temporary Residence - Government Contract Worker)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency for foreign nationals contracted to work on specific government-awarded projects in Panama.
visa_types.notes: Tied to the duration of the government contract. Permit lapses when the contract ends.

visa_types.name: Visa de Estudiante (Student Visa / Temporary Resident Student Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Allows foreign nationals to reside in Panama for the duration of an accredited academic program at a recognized Panamanian institution.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a quick-access service on the SNM homepage. Covers standard students as well as exchange program participants and Fulbright scholarship recipients. Permit is tied to enrollment in the educational institution. Specific fees and processing times available via downloadable PDFs on migracion.gob.pa. Executive Decree 3 of February 6, 2026 created a permanent residency pathway for students after completing their studies.

visa_types.name: Pensionado / Jubilado (Retiree / Pensioner Permanent Residency)
visa_types.purpose: Grants immediate permanent residency to foreign retirees who can demonstrate a lifetime monthly pension income from an official or private pension source.
visa_types.notes: Grants immediate permanent residency (not a temporary visa). Requires proof of lifetime monthly income of at least USD 1,000 from a government pension, social security, military pension, or private pension plan. Income threshold reduces to USD 750/month if applicant purchases Panamanian real estate worth at least USD 100,000. Available to anyone 18 years or older regardless of nationality. Path to citizenship after 5 years of permanent residency. Widely considered one of Panama's most popular residency programs. Accompanied by significant discount benefits (Pensionado card) on healthcare, transport, hotels, and entertainment.

visa_types.name: Visa de Inversionista Calificado (Qualified Investor Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Grants immediate permanent residency to foreign nationals making a qualifying investment in Panama via real estate, securities, or fixed-term bank deposit.
visa_types.notes: Established by Decreto Ejecutivo 722 (amended by Decrees 109 and 193 of 2024). Grants immediate permanent residency upon approval. Three investment modalities: (1) Real estate: USD 300,000 minimum (valid until October 15, 2026, then increases to USD 500,000); (2) Securities/stocks on Panama Stock Exchange: USD 500,000 minimum for at least 5 years; (3) Fixed-term bank deposit: USD 750,000 minimum for at least 5 years. Investment must originate from abroad and be maintained for at least 5 years. Processing approximately 30 business days. After 5 years of permanent residency, eligible for naturalization. Administered by SNM and MICI.

visa_types.name: Residencia por Solvencia Económica (Economic Solvency Residence)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary then permanent residency for foreign nationals who demonstrate economic solvency through investment in Panamanian real estate or fixed-term bank deposits.
visa_types.notes: Requires investment of at least USD 300,000 in real estate and/or a Panamanian bank fixed-term deposit (or mixed investment). Grants 2-year temporary residency first, then permanent residency after 2 years. Three sub-options: fixed-term deposit, real estate investment, or mixed investment. Available to all nationalities. Citizenship pathway after 5 years of permanent residency.

visa_types.name: Naciones Amigas / Países Específicos (Friendly Nations Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent residency for nationals of approximately 50 'friendly nations' who can demonstrate economic ties to Panama.
visa_types.notes: Established by Executive Decree 226 of July 20, 2021. Open to nationals of approximately 50 'friendly' nations. Requires proof of economic ties to Panama (employment with a Panamanian company, ownership of a Panamanian business, or real estate purchase) and a bank balance of at least USD 5,000 in a Panamanian bank. Grants permanent residency. After 5 years continuous permanent residency, eligible for naturalization. Eligible nationalities include: Germany, Andorra, Argentina, Australia, Austria, Belgium, Brazil, Canada, Chile, Cyprus, South Korea, Costa Rica, Croatia, Denmark, Slovakia, Spain, USA, Estonia, Finland, France, Greece, Hong Kong, Hungary, Ireland, Israel, Japan, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, San Marino, Mexico, Monaco, Montenegro, Norway, New Zealand, Netherlands, Paraguay, Poland, Portugal, UK, Czech Republic, Serbia, Singapore, South Africa, Sweden, Switzerland, Taiwan, Uruguay (approx. 50 nations per Decree 226 of 2021).

visa_types.name: Permiso de Residente Permanente por Matrimonio con Panameño/a (Permanent Residency - Spouse of Panamanian National)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent residency for foreign nationals legally married to a Panamanian citizen.
visa_types.notes: Also covers parents of Panamanian children and dependents of permanent residents under the family reunification categories listed by SNM. Processing handled by SNM. Specific fees and required documents available via downloadable PDFs on migracion.gob.pa.

visa_types.name: Reunificación Familiar (Family Regrouping Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary residency permit that allows qualifying family members (dependents) of legally resident foreign nationals to join them in Panama.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a temporary resident permit category on the SNM Permisos Migratorios page. Covers dependents (spouse, children) of foreign nationals who already hold a valid temporary or permanent residency in Panama.

visa_types.name: Visa de Corta Estancia - Tratamiento Médico (Short-Stay Medical Treatment Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visa for foreign nationals traveling to Panama specifically to receive medical treatment at a Panamanian healthcare facility.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a short-stay non-resident visa category on the SNM Permisos Migratorios page. Specific fees and documentation requirements available in downloadable PDFs from migracion.gob.pa. Typically requires a letter from the treating Panamanian medical institution.

visa_types.name: Visa Diplomática / Exención para Pasaporte Diplomático (Diplomatic Visa / Diplomatic Passport Exemption)
visa_types.purpose: Visa-free or facilitated entry for holders of diplomatic passports under bilateral agreements, and visas for diplomatic personnel not covered by exemptions.
visa_types.notes: Diplomatic passport holders of Cuba, Dominican Republic, Haiti, Indonesia, Philippines, Republic of China (Taiwan), and Venezuela receive visa-free entry despite those nationalities' ordinary passports requiring visas. Diplomatic personnel accredited to Panama receive MIRE-coordinated courtesy visas. Special commercial/investor visas and former diplomat categories also exist under SNM permit system.

visa_types.name: Permiso de Residente Permanente - Regularización Migratoria 'Crisol de Razas' (General Migration Regularization)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent residency pathway for foreign nationals already residing in Panama who do not meet standard permit requirements, provided they meet humanitarian or demographic criteria.
visa_types.notes: Periodic amnesty/regularization programs. The 'Crisol de Razas' category represents Panama's historical immigration policy welcoming migrants of diverse backgrounds. Activated periodically by executive decree. Not a continuously available visa type.

visa_types.name: Protección Humanitaria (Humanitarian Protection / Asylum)
visa_types.purpose: Temporary humanitarian protection for foreign nationals who cannot safely return to their home country, including asylum seekers and refugees.
visa_types.notes: Panama is a signatory to the 1951 Refugee Convention. Refugee/asylum applications are processed by the National Office for Refugees (ONPAR) under MIRE. Short-stay humanitarian assistance visa is also listed as a non-resident category on the SNM page.

visa_types.name: Permiso Especial de Permanencia - Zona Franca de Colón / Panama Pacífico (Special Economic Zone Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Permanent or temporary residency for investors, executives, and workers operating within Panama's special economic zones including the Colón Free Zone and Panama Pacífico.
visa_types.notes: Separate permit regimes exist for the Colón Free Zone, Panama Pacífico Special Economic Area, export processing zones, and film/audiovisual industry investors. Panama Pacífico is governed by Law 41 of 2004. Specific investment and employment thresholds apply per zone.

visa_free.notes: Up to 180 days as tourists under Resolution 22706 (reciprocity basis). Also listed as qualifying third-country visa issuer under Executive Decree 521.
Up to 180 days as tourists under Resolution 22706 (reciprocity basis). Also listed as qualifying third-country visa issuer under Executive Decree 521.

Visa-free up to 30 days for HKSAR passport holders under a bilateral mutual visa-free arrangement effective February 10, 2019. Confirmed by Hong Kong Immigration Department (IMMD) press release of February 1, 2019.

visa_policy.notes: Panama does not operate an e-visa or ETA system. Visas (stamped or authorized) must be obtained from consulates for nationalities not covered by exemptions. The visa-free list is sourced from the Panama Consulate in California (panaconsul.net), which is an official consulate representation; the specific decrees referenced (Resolution 22706 and Executive Decree 521) are issued by the Ministerio de Seguridad Publica / SNM. USA and Canada nationals receive up to 180 days; other exempt nationalities receive up to 90 days. Executive Decree 196 of October 28, 2024 (Gaceta Oficial No. 30149-D) modified Article 2 of Executive Decree 521 of August 6, 2018, removing the prior requirements that the third-country visa be multiple-entry, previously used in the issuing country, and valid for at least 6 months - now only a valid visa from an approved country is required.

conditional_access.conditions: Traveller must hold a valid visa issued by the United States. As of Executive Decree 196 of October 28, 2024 (Gaceta Oficial No. 30149-D, modifying Article 2 of Executive Decree 521 of August 6, 2018), the prior requirements of multiple-entry, prior use in the issuing country, and minimum 6-month validity are all removed; any valid visa is sufficient. Passport must have minimum 3 months validity. Proof of economic solvency of at least USD 500 required. Round-trip ticket required.

Migration Officers explicitly accept a valid US permanent residence card (Green Card / Form I-551) under Executive Decree 521 (as amended by Decree 196 of October 28, 2024). Passport must have minimum 3 months validity. Proof of economic solvency of at least USD 500 required. Round-trip ticket required.

Traveller must hold a valid visa issued by Canada. As of Executive Decree 196 of October 28, 2024 (modifying Executive Decree 521 of August 6, 2018), prior-use, multiple-entry, and 6-month validity requirements are removed; any valid visa is sufficient. Passport must have minimum 3 months validity. Proof of economic solvency of at least USD 500 required. Round-trip ticket required.

Traveller must hold a valid visa issued by the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland. As of Executive Decree 196 of October 28, 2024 (modifying Executive Decree 521 of August 6, 2018), prior-use, multiple-entry, and 6-month validity requirements are removed; any valid visa is sufficient. Passport must have minimum 3 months validity. Proof of economic solvency of at least USD 500 required. Round-trip ticket required.

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen visa (any EU member state)

conditional_access.conditions: Traveller must hold a valid visa issued by any of the states that make up the European Union. As of Executive Decree 196 of October 28, 2024 (modifying Executive Decree 521 of August 6, 2018), prior-use, multiple-entry, and 6-month validity requirements are removed; any valid visa is sufficient. Passport must have minimum 3 months validity. Proof of economic solvency of at least USD 500 required. Round-trip ticket required.

Traveller must hold a valid visa issued by the Commonwealth of Australia. As of Executive Decree 196 of October 28, 2024 (modifying Executive Decree 521 of August 6, 2018), prior-use, multiple-entry, and 6-month validity requirements are removed; any valid visa is sufficient. Passport must have minimum 3 months validity. Proof of economic solvency of at least USD 500 required. Round-trip ticket required.

Traveller must hold a valid visa issued by the State of Japan. As of Executive Decree 196 of October 28, 2024 (modifying Executive Decree 521 of August 6, 2018), prior-use, multiple-entry, and 6-month validity requirements are removed; any valid visa is sufficient. Passport must have minimum 3 months validity. Proof of economic solvency of at least USD 500 required. Round-trip ticket required.

Traveller must hold a valid visa issued by the Republic of Korea. As of Executive Decree 196 of October 28, 2024 (modifying Executive Decree 521 of August 6, 2018), prior-use, multiple-entry, and 6-month validity requirements are removed; any valid visa is sufficient. Passport must have minimum 3 months validity. Proof of economic solvency of at least USD 500 required. Round-trip ticket required.

Traveller must hold a valid visa issued by the Republic of Singapore. As of Executive Decree 196 of October 28, 2024 (modifying Executive Decree 521 of August 6, 2018), prior-use, multiple-entry, and 6-month validity requirements are removed; any valid visa is sufficient. Passport must have minimum 3 months validity. Proof of economic solvency of at least USD 500 required. Round-trip ticket required.
Diplomatic passport holders of Cuba do not require a visa to enter Panama. Ordinary passport holders of Cuba require a stamped visa.
Diplomatic passport holders of the Dominican Republic do not require a visa to enter Panama. Ordinary passport holders of the Dominican Republic require a stamped visa.
Diplomatic passport holders of Haiti do not require a visa to enter Panama. Ordinary passport holders of Haiti require an authorized visa.
Diplomatic passport holders of Indonesia do not require a visa to enter Panama. Ordinary passport holders of Indonesia require an authorized visa.
Diplomatic passport holders of the Philippines do not require a visa to enter Panama. Ordinary passport holders of the Philippines require a stamped visa.
Diplomatic passport holders of the Republic of China (Taiwan) do not require a visa to enter Panama. Ordinary passport holders of Taiwan are visa-free under Resolution 22706.
Diplomatic passport holders of Venezuela do not require a visa to enter Panama. Ordinary passport holders of Venezuela require a stamped visa.

cbi.notes: Panama does not offer direct citizenship-by-investment. The pathway is: invest to obtain permanent residency under the Qualified Investor Visa (Decreto Ejecutivo 722), then apply for naturalization after 5 years of permanent residency. This is a residency-by-investment program with a path to citizenship, not a direct CBI. Dual citizenship status under Panamanian law was not confirmed on official sources.

programs.name: Qualified Investor Visa (Visa de Inversionista Calificado)
programs.notes: Established by Decreto Ejecutivo 722 (and subsequent amendments including Decreto 109 and Decreto 193 of 2024). Grants immediate permanent residency upon approval. Three investment modalities: (1) Real estate: USD 300,000 minimum (valid until October 15, 2026, then increases to USD 500,000); (2) Securities/stocks on Panama Stock Exchange: USD 500,000 minimum for at least 5 years; (3) Fixed-term bank deposit: USD 750,000 minimum for at least 5 years. Investment must originate from abroad and be maintained for at least 5 years. Processing: approximately 30 business days. After 5 years of permanent residency, eligible to apply for Panamanian naturalization/citizenship with minimal physical presence requirement (one visit to Panama every two years suffices). Administered by SNM (National Migration Service) and MICI (Ministry of Commerce and Industries).

programs.name: Friendly Nations Visa (Visa de Paises Especificos / Naciones Amigas)
programs.notes: Established by Executive Decree 226 of July 20, 2021. Open to nationals of approximately 50 'friendly' nations (naciones amigas). Requires proof of economic ties to Panama (employment with a Panamanian company, own a Panamanian business, or real estate purchase) and a bank balance of at least USD 5,000 in a Panamanian bank. Grants permanent residency. After 5 years continuous permanent residency, eligible for naturalization. Eligible nationalities include: Germany, Andorra, Argentina, Australia, Austria, Belgium, Brazil, Canada, Chile, Cyprus, South Korea, Costa Rica, Croatia, Denmark, Slovakia, Spain, United States, Estonia, Finland, France, Greece, Hong Kong, Hungary, Ireland, Israel, Japan, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, San Marino, Mexico, Monaco, Montenegro, Norway, New Zealand, Netherlands, Paraguay, Poland, Portugal, United Kingdom, Czech Republic, Serbia, Singapore, South Africa, Sweden, Switzerland, Taiwan, Uruguay (approximately 50 nations total per Decree 226 of 2021). Note: The official PDF at migracion.gob.pa could not be fully parsed at time of research.

programs.name: Economic Solvency Residence (Residencia por Solvencia Economica)
programs.notes: Requires investment of at least USD 300,000 in real estate and/or a Panamanian bank fixed-term deposit (or mixed investment). Grants 2-year temporary residency first, then permanent residency after 2 years. Three sub-options: fixed-term deposit, real estate investment, or mixed investment. Available to all nationalities. Citizenship pathway after 5 years of permanent residency.

programs.name: Pensionado / Jubilado Visa (Retiree / Pensioner Residence)
programs.notes: Grants immediate permanent residency. Requires proof of lifetime monthly income of at least USD 1,000 from a government pension, social security, military pension, or private pension plan. The income threshold reduces to USD 750/month if the applicant purchases real estate in Panama worth at least USD 100,000. Available to anyone 18 years or older. Widely considered one of Panama's most popular residency programs.

programs.name: Agricultural / Forestry Investor Residence
programs.notes: Permanent or temporary residency category for investors in agriculture or forestry sectors. Specific minimum investment amounts not found on official source pages at time of research.

programs.name: Short-Stay Visa as a Remote Worker (Visa de Corta Estancia como Trabajador Remoto)
programs.eligibility: Must work for a foreign transnational company or be a self-employed remote worker performing functions with effects abroad; minimum annual income of B/. 36,000 (USD 36,000) from foreign sources; must not work for Panamanian companies or clients inside Panama.
programs.processing_time: Applied at Special Processing Window of SNM; applicant must already be in Panama territory. Processing time not specified on official sources.
programs.notes: Created by Executive Decree 198 of May 7, 2021. Grants right to work remotely in Panama. Maximum initial stay of 9 months, renewable once for a further 9 months (total 18 months maximum). Applicant must be present in Panama to apply. Also referenced on ProPanama official investment promotion agency site (propanama.gob.pa). Foreign-source income is not subject to Panamanian income tax under Panama's territorial tax system.

agreements: Central America-4 (CA-4) Border Control Agreement (Panama is not a CA-4 member; it has bilateral visa-free arrangements with Central American CA-4 countries)
Panama Canal Neutrality Treaty (bilateral with USA - not a migration/freedom-of-movement agreement)

validation_notes: ADDED: Russia (RUS) to visa_free list at 90 days - confirmed on panaconsul.net (official consulate) list and corroborated by Wikipedia citing a decree of 14 April 2014. Prior record omitted this entry.

REMOVED: 'British National Overseas (BNO)' from visa_free - BNO is a UK-issued passport status/type, not a separate nationality. BNO holders travel on UK passports and are correctly captured under the GBR (United Kingdom) entry. Having a separate BNO entry was a classification error.

CORRECTED: conditional_access conditions text for all 9 third-country visa credential entries - prior text said Decree 196 removed 'multiple-entry and prior use' requirements but omitted that the minimum 6-month validity requirement was also removed. Corrected to state that all three prior requirements (multiple-entry, prior use, 6-month minimum validity) are removed; only a valid visa is now required. Confirmed via Gaceta Oficial No. 30149-D of October 28, 2024 and corroborated by lemoslegal.com blog summarizing the decree.
CORRECTED: 'Macedonia (North Macedonia)' name updated to 'North Macedonia' (the official internationally recognised name since 2019).
ADDED: embassyofpanama.org to official_domains list - it was already used as source_url in conditional_access entries but was missing from the official_domains array.
NO CHANGE: Decree Executive 3 of February 6, 2026 (student permanent residency) does not affect visa-free entry policy.

NO CHANGE: 2025 decrees (Resolutions 1191, 4887, 6616, 7533, 7534; Decree 6) cover humanitarian permits, regularisation, and Venezuelan passport recognition - none affect the visa-free nationality list.
VERIFIED: Executive Decree 196 of October 28, 2024 modifies Article 2 of Decree 521 - confirmed correct in prior record; conditions text updated to reflect all three removed requirements.
VERIFIED: USA and Canada 180-day limit under Resolution 22706 - confirmed correct.
VERIFIED: No e-visa or ETA system - confirmed correct, Panama still has no such system.
VERIFIED: CBI has_program=false - confirmed correct; no direct citizenship-by-investment program exists.

ADDED: visa_types array populated with 20 visa/permit categories sourced from migracion.gob.pa/permisos-migratorios/, embassyofpanama.org/visas-1, mici.gob.pa, and migracion.gob.pa remote worker PDF. Covers: tourist (visa-free, stamped, authorized), transit, business short-stay, digital nomad, work (V-TET, multinational, government contract), student, retirement (pensionado), investment (qualified investor, economic solvency, friendly nations), family (spouse/dependents, reunification), medical short-stay, diplomatic, regularization, humanitarian protection, and special economic zone permits.

[2026-06-27] ADDED: Hong Kong SAR (HKG) to visa_free list at 30 days - confirmed by Hong Kong Immigration Department (IMMD) official press release dated February 1, 2019 (https://www.immd.gov.hk/eng/press/press-releases/20190201b.html). Mutual bilateral visa-free arrangement effective February 10, 2019. Previously noted in data_quality.gaps as unconfirmed but now verified from official HK government source.

[2026-06-29] ADDED: Latvia (LVA) to visa_free array at 90 days - Latvia is an EU member state; the Embassy of Panama (embassyofpanama.org/visas-1) re-confirmed in this run that all EU member state nationals are visa-free. Latvia was missing from the visa_free array (the prior 2026-06-27 run wrote a note claiming it was added but did not actually insert the array entry). All 27 EU member states are now present.

[2026-06-27] VERIFIED: No changes to visa-free nationality list in 2025. All 2025 decrees reviewed (Resolutions 1191, 4887; Executive Decrees 6, 26, 21, 22) affect only regularisation, temporary permits, identification requirements, and transit policy - none modify the visa-free entry list.
[2026-06-27] VERIFIED: Executive Decree 26 (November 11, 2025) modifies ID presentation requirements for foreigners already inside Panama; does not affect visa-free tourist entry policy.

[2026-06-27] VERIFIED: Decrees 21 and 22 (August 25, 2025) affect only transit visa rules for Haitian and Cuban nationals respectively - Decree 21 exempts Haitians from transit visa; Decree 22 requires Cubans to obtain transit visa. These are transit-only rules, not tourist entry policy changes. tourist entry for both nationalities remains unchanged (visa required for both).

[2026-06-27] VERIFIED: Angola (AGO), Cape Verde (CPV), Gabon (GAB), Georgia (GEO), Kenya (KEN) - correctly absent from visa_free list. These five countries had their visa exemptions cancelled by Resolution 16024 of June 8, 2023 and Resolution 24430 of August 9, 2023.
[2026-06-27] VERIFIED: No new e-visa or ETA system introduced by Panama in 2025 or 2026 - confirmed by review of all 2025 and 2026 decrees.

[2026-06-29] VERIFIED: Re-fetched panaconsul.net/visas, embassyofpanama.org/visas-1, migracion.gob.pa/turistas/, migracion.gob.pa/decretos-y-resoluciones-2025/, migracion.gob.pa/decretos-y-resoluciones-2026/. No changes to visa-free list in 2025 or 2026. 2026 decree list contains only Decree 3 (student permanent residency, Feb 2026). Belarus, Russia, Turkey, Cambodia, Bhutan, Mongolia, Armenia, Madagascar, Comoros all confirmed as 90-day visa-free. No new ETA or e-visa system. No suspended agreements found.

[2026-06-29] CORRECTED: Latvia (LVA) array entry inserted (the 2026-06-27 note claimed the entry was added but the actual JSON array entry was absent). Latvia is now correctly present in visa_free at 90 days, sourced from embassyofpanama.org/visas-1 (EU member nationals confirmed visa-free). Total visa_free entries now 114.

[2026-06-29] VERIFIED: São Tomé and Príncipe (STP) - Wikipedia lists as visa-free 90 days, but official Panama sources (panaconsul.net, embassyofpanama.org, migracion.gob.pa) do not list it. Safety rule: classify DOWN. STP remains absent from visa_free, kept in gaps.
gaps: Exact text of Resolution 22706 establishing the full visa-free list was not directly accessible; list sourced from Panama Consulate in California (panaconsul.net) and Embassy of Panama (embassyofpanama.org) which are official representations but not the primary SNM/MIRE portal.

The official PDF for the Friendly Nations Visa (02-PAISES-ESPECIFICOS.pdf at migracion.gob.pa) could not be parsed as plain text due to binary/compressed encoding; country list and requirements sourced from search summaries citing Decree 226 of 2021.
Minimum investment amounts for Agricultural/Forestry Investor Residence programs not found on official sources.
Panama does not operate an e-visa or ETA system - these categories are empty by design.
Visa-on-arrival category is empty - Panama does not have a formal VOA scheme.
Full list of countries exempt only with diplomatic passports (Cuba, Dominican Republic, Haiti, Indonesia, Philippines, Taiwan, Venezuela) may be incomplete; sourced from embassyofpanama.org only.
Confirmation of whether Panama allows dual citizenship not found on official government sources.
Exact processing time for the Friendly Nations Visa not found on official sources.

Panama's freedom-of-movement agreements: Panama is not a member of CARICOM or any formal common travel area; it has bilateral visa-suppression agreements but no multilateral FOM bloc membership confirmed on official sources.

MIRE treaty sub-pages listing full country-by-country visa suppression agreements (mire.gob.pa/ministerio/tratados/) return HTTP 404; complete list of all bilateral diplomatic/ordinary passport suppression agreements could not be confirmed from official sources.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) or comparable special diaspora/heritage status entry rule found in any official Panama source.

Whether residence permits (not just visas) from Canada, UK, Australia, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, and EU member states (beyond the explicitly confirmed US Green Card) qualify under the amended Decree 521 was not re-confirmed on the official Embassy page after the October 2024 amendment; the page explicitly calls out only 'valid visa' and 'US permanent residence card'.

Sao Tome and Principe (STP): embassies.net lists this country as 90-day visa-free for Panama, and Wikipedia's visa policy of Panama article also lists it; however this could not be confirmed on panaconsul.net, embassyofpanama.org, or any official Panama government domain; not added to visa_free pending official confirmation.
Hong Kong (HKG) RESOLVED: Entry confirmed at 30 days via IMMD official press release - added to visa_free list in 2026-06-27 update.
Latvia (LVA) RESOLVED: Visa_free array entry confirmed inserted in 2026-06-29 run (the 2026-06-27 note claiming it was added was erroneous - entry was absent). Now confirmed present.

The real estate minimum for the Qualified Investor Visa is scheduled to increase from USD 300,000 to USD 500,000 after October 15, 2026 - this date-specific change should be re-verified once past the threshold date.
Specific fees for most visa_types entries are not publicly displayed on official SNM web pages; they are contained in downloadable PDFs which returned binary data and could not be parsed.
Processing times for most visa_types categories not published on official web pages; PDF documents also not parseable.
Working holiday visa: Panama has no confirmed working holiday agreement with any country; this category was not documented.
